summary: Large allosauroid carnivore — 'shark-toothed lizard.' Comparable in mass class to other island megapredators; assigned priority recovery due to genome rarity.
facts: Serrated, blade-like teeth — adapted for slicing flesh from large prey.
Skull length exceeds 1.5 m in mature specimens.
May compete directly with Tyrannosaur assets for territory.
Embryo cryo-state stable; genetic drift within acceptable parameters.
fieldNotes: Maintain minimum 200 m standoff. Track downwind — olfactory sensitivity elevated.
